description_long: This object is a modern, twin-engine jet airplane with a sleek, aerodynamic fuselage and sharply swept wings. The wings are mid-mounted and feature a pronounced sweep, giving the aircraft a streamlined, high-speed appearance. The tail section includes twin vertical stabilizers set at an outward angle, connected by a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration. The engines are mounted at the rear of the fuselage, integrated closely with the body for reduced drag. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely designed for one or two crew members. The surface is smooth and metallic, with a uniform gray coloration and no visible landing gear in the current configuration.
description: A sleek, twin-engine jet airplane with swept mid-mounted wings, twin vertical stabilizers, and a streamlined gray metallic body.
description_view_0: Side profile showing the elongated fuselage, sharply swept wings, and twin vertical stabilizers at the rear.
description_view_1: Rear top-down view highlighting the twin engines, H-tail configuration, and the full span of the swept wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, again showing the streamlined body, cockpit placement, and wing sweep.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the pointed nose, cockpit canopy, and the symmetry of the wings and twin tail fins.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with sharply swept wings and no visible winglets; wings are integrated smoothly into the fuselage with a slight anhedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted at the rear fuselage, closely integrated with the body.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin outward-canted vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer connecting them.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No landing gear visible in the current images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit integrated into the nose of the fuselage, likely single or tandem seat with a streamlined canopy.
